result.stdout:
Description:
  DeadSharp: A tool for analyzing C# projects to identify dead code

Usage:
  DeadSharp [options]

Options:
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)    Path to the project or solution to analyze
  -v, --verbose                   Enable verbose output for detailed analysis information
  -i, --ignore-tests              Ignore test projects during analysis
  -im, --ignore-migrations        Ignore database migration files during analysis
  -iaf, --ignore-azure-functions  Ignore Azure Function files during analysis
  -ic, --ignore-controllers       Ignore Controller files during analysis
  -ed, --enhanced-di-detection    Enable enhanced dependency injection detection to better identify classes used only in DI containers
  -edf, --enhanced-dataflow       Enable enhanced data flow analysis using deep semantic analysis to detect complex usage patterns through indirect references, factory patterns, and control flow tracking
  -o, --output <output>           Path to save the analysis results in JSON format
  -f, --format <format>           Output format for results (JSON or TXT). Default is console if no format is specified. [default: console]
  --version                       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information
